name: ssh-ops
description: MCP server and CLI for remote Linux server operations: inventory, health, disk, file ops, service control, log search, network checks, package management, cron CRUD, user management, permissions, sudoers, dynamic profile management, encrypted passwords, and SSH -J jump chain management.

interface.shortDescription: 27 SSH tools: inventory, files, services, packages, cron, users, permissions, sudoers, network, IP assignment, profiles, jump chain.
interface.longDescription: SSH Ops exposes 27 MCP tools for remote Linux server operations: hardware inventory, health checks, disk reports, log search, network probing, file read/write/patch (binary-safe), systemd service control, package management (apt/yum/dnf/apk auto-detect), crontab CRUD, user management (add/del/mod/passwd/lock), chmod+chown+chgrp, sudoers management via /etc/sudoers.d/, parallel multi-host execution, IP address assignment (permanent, all network stacks), dynamic profile management with AES-256-GCM encrypted passwords, SSH -J jump chain management, localSwitchUser for bastion-local execution, and SSH key discovery.
defaultPrompt: Check my server hardware with SSH Ops.
Run a health check across all my servers.
Install nginx on my production server.
Search nginx logs for errors in the last hour.
Add a daily backup cron job on my server.
Can my app server reach the database on port 5432?
Add a new server with password login to SSH Ops profiles.
Remove the staging server from SSH Ops profiles.
